---
title: "Week 5 — Exploratory Data Analysis I: Distributions, Shape, Spread, Outliers"
---

## Learning Objectives

By the end of this session, you will be able to:

- Compute and interpret measures of center (mean, median, mode) for clinical data
- Compute and interpret measures of spread (range, IQR, standard deviation, variance)
- Describe distribution shape: symmetric, skewed left/right, unimodal/bimodal
- Identify and handle outliers: understand when they are errors vs. real clinical signal
- Choose appropriate summaries based on data type (continuous vs. ordinal)
- Produce a complete descriptive summary of a clinical dataset in a Quarto document

## Why EDA Matters Before Analysis

Before running any statistical test, you must understand your data's distribution. The distribution tells you:

- **Which test is valid** — t-test assumes approximately normal; skewed data needs non-parametric alternatives
- **Where the data came from** — a bimodal distribution might indicate two patient populations
- **What is suspicious** — an outlier could be a data entry error (weight = 700 kg) or a real clinical event (weight = 12 kg — neonate in adult ICU data)
- **How to report it** — mean ± SD for symmetric; median [IQR] for skewed

**EDA is not optional. It is the step that prevents you from reporting meaningless statistics.**

## Core Concepts

### Measures of Center

| Measure | Best For | R Function | Clinical Example |
|---------|----------|------------|------------------|
| **Mean** | Symmetric, continuous data | `mean()` | Mean MAP in a stable cohort |
| **Median** | Skewed data, ordinal data | `median()` | Median ICU LOS (right-skewed: most short, some very long) |
| **Mode** | Categorical/nominal data | `table()` then max | Most common diagnosis |

**Rule:** For skewed continuous data (common in clinical data), always report median and IQR, not mean and SD.

### Measures of Spread

| Measure | Best For | R Function | Clinical Example |
|---------|----------|------------|------------------|
| **Range** | Quick check; vulnerable to outliers | `range()` or `max() - min()` | Age range in study |
| **IQR** | Robust measure for skewed data | `IQR()` | IQR of creatinine — not affected by one patient on dialysis |
| **Standard Deviation** | Symmetric, normal-ish data | `sd()` | SD of MAP in controlled setting |
| **Variance** | Mathematical foundation; rarely reported | `var()` | Used in calculations, not tables |

**The 1.5 × IQR rule for outliers:** Values below Q1 - 1.5×IQR or above Q3 + 1.5×IQR are flagged. This is a convention, not a clinical judgment.

### Distribution Shape

| Shape | Description | Common in Clinical Data | Example |
|-------|-------------|------------------------|---------|
| **Symmetric** | Mean ≈ median; mirror image | Height, BMI in homogeneous population | — |
| **Right-skewed** | Tail extends right; mean > median | Length of stay, cost, waiting times | ICU LOS: most 2-3 days, some 30+ |
| **Left-skewed** | Tail extends left; mean < median | Less common; test scores with ceiling effect | Pain scores near 0 (floor effect creates left skew) |
| **Bimodal** | Two peaks | Mixed populations | Hospital-wide age: pediatric peak + adult peak |
| **Uniform** | Flat; all values equally likely | Rare in biology | Randomized treatment assignment |

### Outliers: Error vs. Signal

**Critical clinical skill:** Distinguishing data entry errors from genuine extreme values.

| Scenario | Action | Example |
|----------|--------|---------|
| Clearly impossible | Remove and document | Heart rate = 500; weight = 700 kg |
| Extreme but physiologically possible | Keep; investigate | MAP = 40 (shock), MAP = 150 (hypertensive crisis) |
| Unusual combination | Keep; may be signal | ASA V patient in elective surgery dataset |
| Same value repeated | Check instrument | SpO2 = 100.0 for 6 hours straight (probe may be off) |

**Never silently delete outliers.** Document every removal with clinical rationale.

## Exercise

### Part 1: Create a Simulated Clinical Dataset

Create a dataset of 20 patients with the following variables. Use base R only (no packages needed).

```r
# Set seed for reproducibility
set.seed(42)

# Patient IDs
patient_id <- 101:120

# Age: approximately normal, mean 55, SD 15
age <- round(rnorm(20, mean = 55, sd = 15))
age <- pmax(age, 18)  # Minimum age 18
age <- pmin(age, 90)  # Maximum age 90

# Propofol induction dose (mg): weight-based (2 mg/kg), with some variation
# First create weights
weight_kg <- round(rnorm(20, mean = 70, sd = 15))
weight_kg <- pmax(weight_kg, 40)
weight_kg <- pmin(weight_kg, 120)

# Propofol dose with clinical variation (some underdose, some overdose)
propofol_mg <- round(weight_kg * rnorm(20, mean = 2.0, sd = 0.3))

# Add one outlier: data entry error (weight recorded in pounds, not kg)
# Patient 110: weight entered as 154 (pounds = 70 kg), but propofol calculated as if kg
weight_kg[10] <- 154  # Data entry error

# ASA class: mostly II-III, some IV
asa <- sample(c("I", "II", "II", "II", "III", "III", "III", "IV"), 20, replace = TRUE)

# Case duration (minutes): right-skewed (most short, some very long)
case_duration <- round(rlnorm(20, meanlog = 4.5, sdlog = 0.8))

# Combine into data frame
patients <- data.frame(
  patient_id = patient_id,
  age = age,
  weight_kg = weight_kg,
  propofol_mg = propofol_mg,
  asa = ordered(asa, levels = c("I", "II", "III", "IV", "V")),
  case_duration_min = case_duration
)

patients
```

### Part 2: Summarize Each Variable

For each continuous variable (`age`, `weight_kg`, `propofol_mg`, `case_duration_min`), compute:

```r
# Example for age
summary(patients$age)
mean(patients$age)
median(patients$age)
sd(patients$age)
IQR(patients$age)
range(patients$age)

# Check skew: mean vs. median
mean(patients$age) - median(patients$age)
```

For `asa` (ordinal), compute:

```r
table(patients$asa)
median(patients$asa)  # Valid for ordered factors
```

**Document your findings in your Quarto document.** For each variable, note:
- Mean vs. median: is the data skewed?
- Are there obvious outliers?
- What would you report in a paper (mean±SD or median [IQR])?

### Part 3: Identify and Investigate the Outlier

You know there is one data entry error in the dataset (patient 110's weight). Find it using summary statistics:

```r
# Check weight distribution
summary(patients$weight_kg)

# Flag outliers using 1.5 × IQR rule
Q1 <- quantile(patients$weight_kg, 0.25)
Q3 <- quantile(patients$weight_kg, 0.75)
IQR_val <- IQR(patients$weight_kg)

lower_bound <- Q1 - 1.5 * IQR_val
upper_bound <- Q3 + 1.5 * IQR_val

patients$weight_kg[patients$weight_kg < lower_bound | patients$weight_kg > upper_bound]
```

**Questions:**
1. Which patient is flagged as an outlier?
2. What is the most likely explanation (clinical extreme vs. data entry error)?
3. What should you do with this value for analysis? What should you do with it for reporting?

### Part 4: Dose-Weight Relationship

Investigate whether propofol dose is reasonably related to weight:

```r
# Calculate mg/kg for each patient
patients$mg_per_kg <- patients$propofol_mg / patients$weight_kg

summary(patients$mg_per_kg)

# Which patient has the extreme mg/kg ratio?
patients$patient_id[which.max(patients$mg_per_kg)]
```

**Question:** Does this confirm your outlier diagnosis? What would the correct weight be if the entry was in pounds?

### Part 5: Report a Table of Descriptive Statistics

Create a summary table in your Quarto document with the following structure:

| Variable | Type | n | Mean (SD) or Median [IQR] | Range | Notes |
|----------|------|---|---------------------------|-------|-------|
| Age | Continuous | 20 | ? | ? | ? |
| Weight (kg) | Continuous | 20 | ? | ? | ? |
| Propofol dose (mg) | Continuous | 20 | ? | ? | ? |
| ASA class | Ordinal | 20 | ? | ? | ? |
| Case duration (min) | Continuous | 20 | ? | ? | ? |

For continuous variables, choose mean±SD **or** median [IQR] based on distribution shape. Justify your choice.

### Part 6: Reflection

Write 2–3 sentences:

> Why is it dangerous to report "mean ± SD" for every continuous variable without checking the distribution first? Describe a clinical scenario where this would mislead readers.

---

## Common Pitfalls

| Pitfall | Why It Happens | How to Prevent |
|---------|----------------|----------------|
| "I reported mean ± SD for ICU LOS" | Mean ± SD is the default in many templates | Always check skew: `mean > median` → right-skewed → report median [IQR] |
| "I deleted the outlier without documenting it" | Outliers make analysis "messy" | Document: "Patient 110 excluded: weight recorded in pounds, confirmed by mg/kg ratio" |
| "I assumed normal distribution because the sample size is large" | Misunderstanding Central Limit Theorem | CLT applies to sampling distribution of the mean, not the data itself. Check histogram. |
| "I reported 3 significant figures for n=20" | Default R output | Round appropriately: for n=20, one decimal place for means is sufficient |
| "I forgot to set.seed()" | Results change every render | Always `set.seed()` before `rnorm()`, `sample()`, etc. for reproducibility |

## Verification

Before leaving this session, confirm you can:

- [ ] Generate reproducible random data with `set.seed()`
- [ ] Compute mean, median, SD, IQR, and range for continuous variables
- [ ] Determine whether data is symmetric or skewed using mean vs. median
- [ ] Identify outliers using the 1.5 × IQR rule
- [ ] Distinguish data entry errors from genuine clinical extremes
- [ ] Choose appropriate summary statistics based on distribution shape
- [ ] Produce a clean summary table in a Quarto document
- [ ] Push your document to GitHub

## Resources

- **R for Data Science (2e):** [r4ds.hadley.nz/eda.html](https://r4ds.hadley.nz/eda.html) — EDA chapter
- **Describing Distributions:** [Khan Academy — Statistics and Probability](https://www.khanacademy.org/math/statistics-probability)
- **Propofol Dosing:** Miller's Anesthesia, 9th ed. — Standard induction dose 1.5–2.5 mg/kg IV
- **ASA Physical Status:** [ASA House of Delegates](https://www.asahq.org/standards-and-practice-parameters/statement-on-asa-physical-status-classification-system)

---

[← Back to Basic Tier](../index.qmd)
